mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in PAYEMS signals broad income and spending support for corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when payroll momentum improves.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in HOUST signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and lower future construction activity, reducing cyclicals earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST downside crossings are followed by nonnegative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, markets price future policy tightening and lower real income, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its trailing 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS is below 0.50
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL acceleration and low FEDFUNDS interaction does not produce lower 20-day returns than either variable alone, or if triggered returns are positive in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Lower 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals weaker production, inventory adjustment, and pressure on operating leverage, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if lower INDPRO 3-month momentum has a nonnegative slope in walk-forward return regressions or if bottom-quintile INDPRO momentum does not underperform top-quintile momentum over 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in RSAFS signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month RSAFS growth has Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window or if high-growth RSAFS months fail to outperform low-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS identifies labor-market deterioration severe enough to damage earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month growth crosses below its trailing 12-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS trigger does not underperform single-series labor signals, or if 60-day returns after triggered events are positive in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in FEDFUNDS marks a tightening impulse that raises discount rates and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month maximum by at least 0.10 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS upward crossings are followed by 20-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional mean in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in GDPC1 growth signals broad demand weakness and downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth crosses below 1.0 percent after being above 1.0 percent in the prior release
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth deceleration triggers are not followed by below-average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the triggered hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.
